enter.speaker: narrator
enter.text: A broad man in a stained apron leans against his counter. He smells of brine and woodsmoke. His hands are scarred from scaling knives, and his smile is the widest thing in this village.

hello.speaker: torben
hello.text: Welcome! Fresh catch — well, fresh-ish. The lake has been... uncooperative. But what I have is good. Smoked, grilled, or stewed — your call.

choices.text: What is wrong with the lake?

shop-talk.speaker: torben
shop-talk.text: Grilled pike — puts the strength back in you. Smoked eel — restores body and mind. And my lake tonic, brewed fresh. Clears what ails you. Take a look.

lake-trouble.speaker: torben
lake-trouble.text: Between you and me, something down there is eating the fish. Not a fish. Something with hands. I pulled up a net last week with finger marks pressed into the rope. Five fingers. Long ones.

lake-trouble-2.speaker: torben
lake-trouble-2.text: Nessa says not to talk about it. Says it scares the last few customers away. She is probably right. But I have been sleeping with a gutting knife under my pillow, and I am not a man who scares easy.

farewell.speaker: torben
farewell.text: Safe travels. And if you are near the water after dark — stay away from the edge.
